Served as Minister of State at the Department for Transport October 2022 to November 2023 and as Financial Secretary to the Treasury May 2019 to September 2021. Appointed Shadow Leader of the House of Commons in November 2024. MP for Hereford and South Herefordshire Serving as Conservative MP for Hereford and South Herefordshire since May 2010. Served as Minister of State at the Department for Transport October 2022 to November 2023 and as Financial Secretary to the Treasury May 2019 to September 2021. Appointed Shadow Leader of the House of Commons in November 2024. MP for Hereford and South Herefordshire Serving as Conservative MP for Hereford and South Herefordshire since May 2010. Served as Minister of State at the Department for Transport October 2022 to November 2023 and as Financial Secretary to the Treasury May 2019 to September 2021. Appointed Shadow Leader of the House of Commons in November 2024. pages.title: About Jesse | Jesse Norman
pages.text_excerpt: About Jesse | Jesse Norman Skip to main content Main navigation Home About In Parliament News Articles Contact Search Cleaning Up the Wye NMITE User account menu Log in Toggle navigation Main navigation Home About My Record In Parliament News Articles Contact Search Cleaning Up the Wye NMITE About Jesse The Rt Hon Jesse Norman has been the Member of Parliament for Hereford and South Herefordshire since 2010. He has been married to Kate since 1992, and they have three children. Wide experience Jesse was educated at Merton College, Oxford University, and has a Masters and PhD from University College London. He worked for and later ran an educational project giving away medical and other textbooks in Eastern Europe from 1988-1991. In 1991 he went to work at Barclays, mainly on eastern Europe and other emerging markets. He left Barclays in 1997 as a Director in order to teach and do research in philosophy at UCL, and later at Birkbeck College. He was an Honorary Fellow at UCL in 2005-10, and a Visiting Fellow at All Soul's College, Oxford in 2016-17. A lifelong volunteer, Jesse has personally raised more than half a million pounds for charity. He has also been a school governor of an inner city comprehensive and worked for nearly a decade with the Roundhouse arts centre and urban regeneration project, which has helped many thousands of disadvantaged young people. He has been a board member of the Hay Festival of Literature and the Arts, and patron of many other local charities. Jesse has been a Conservative since university. He worked to help Oliver Letwin MP retain his seat in West Dorset during the 2005 General Election, and stood as a Conservative candidate in the 2006 Council elections. He has acted as a policy adviser to George Osborne MP, the Shadow Chancellor, and to the Shadow Work and Pensions team. In 2007, he took time out to advise Boris Johnson on his successful campaign to become Mayor of London. He served as Financial Secretary to the Treasury from May 2019 to September 2021 and as such was responsible for tax policy and HM Revenue & Customs, and for the National Infrastructure Strategy, National Infrastructure Commission and Infrastructure Projects Authority. He was very closely involved in the Government's response to the coronavirus pandemic. image/svg+xml Jesse Norman with Chancellor Rishi Sunak and members of the Treasury team on Budget Day 2020. Local campaigner Jesse was selected as a parliamentary candidate in December 2006 in an open primary contest. Since then he has campaigned actively on key local issues, fighting to improve public services and for a stronger voice for Herefordshire in Whitehall and Westminster. His campaigns are regularly featured in the Hereford Times , Hereford Journal and Ross Gazette . Amongst other things, he led a successful campaign to improve local broadband services, was county-wide co-ordinator of the Herefordshire Save Our Post Offices campaign, and set up SchoolsFirst.org.uk as a resource to help all those fighting school closures. He was closely involved in securing Government investment in the Enterprise Zone at Rotherwas, and more recently the successful bid by Hereford for Stronger Towns Fund. Jesse first called for a university in Herefordshire in 2008. He convened the founder team, and has been closely involved with the project ever since, playing an integral role in raising over £23 million in Government and other investment in NMITE, the New Model in Technology and Engineering, as the new university is called. “Jesse Norman has worked tirelessly to support the NHS, particularly to ensure that essential acute hospital services remain available to residents of the County.” Derek Smith, former Chief Executive, Wye Valley NHS Trust High public profile Jesse is a published author of many books and pamphlets, and has written widely in the national press. His book on the life and thought of the great Irish philosopher-statesman Edmund Burke was shortlisted for the Orwell Pr

pages.text_excerpt: In Parliament | Jesse Norman Skip to main content Main navigation Home About In Parliament News Articles Contact Search Cleaning Up the Wye NMITE User account menu Log in Toggle navigation Main navigation Home About In Parliament News Articles Contact Search Cleaning Up the Wye NMITE In Parliament Jesse Norman's contributions in Parliament, speeches, questions and debate interventions. In Parliament Image Jesse Norman welcomes report on access to Parliament and its procedures 16 April 2026 Shadow Leader of the House Jesse Norman welcomes the Modernisation Committee’s report on access to the House of Commons and its Procedures and the administration’s response. Image Jesse Norman demands apology for PM’s contempt of Parliament and grills Government on delayed Defence Investment Plan 16 April 2026 Shadow Leader of the House Jesse Norman highlights the Prime Minister's contemptuous treatment of the Speaker and demands an apology to him and to the House, and he presses the Leader of the House on the fate of the Defence Investment Plan, which he promised WILL be published. Image Jesse Norman urges Government action on Wiggin pension injustice to force trustees to pay top-ups from surpluses 19 March 2026 Jesse Norman urges the Government to intervene in the longstanding injustice perpetrated on pensioners of the Special Metals Wiggin pension fund, by requiring the trustees to use the scheme’s surpluses to provide top-up payments to those affected. Image Jesse Norman urges U-turn on North Sea oil ban to create jobs and cut emissions 19 March 2026 Shadow Leader of the House Jesse Norman criticises the Government’s energy policy as economically and strategically damaging. He calls instead for increased domestic oil and gas production, which he argues would boost jobs, public revenues and national resilience while actually lowering emissions. Image Jesse Norman condemns Government's 'failure of political leadership' on Iran as Gulf allies slam UK’s “slow and weak” response 12 March 2026 Shadow Leader of the House Jesse Norman condemns the failure of political leadership over Iran as our allies in the Gulf criticise this country for its “slow” and “weak” response and the Government attempts to shift the blame to the Chief of Defence Staff. Image Jesse Norman slams PM’s leadership on Iran crisis and improvised legal position 5 March 2026 Shadow Leader of the House Jesse Norman criticises Prime Minister’s leadership over the UK’s response to US and Israeli pre-emptive action against Iran’s nuclear programme. He highlights apparent inconsistencies between the Government’s claim of weeks of preparations and reports of last-minute indecision, and questions whether Ministers are improvising the legal position as they go along. Image Jesse Norman praises We are Farming Minds charity and 142-mile walk for farmers' mental health 12 February 2026 Shadow Leader of the House of Commons Jesse Norman pays tribute to his constituents Sam and Emily Stables, who set up a brilliant charity called We are Farming Minds in Herefordshire and highlights Sam’s 142 mile walk from Ross-on-Wye to London in support of farmers' mental health. Image Jesse Norman pays tribute to Epstein victims and demands transparency on Mandelson vetting presented to PM 5 February 2026 Shadow Leader of the House Jesse Norman pays tribute to the young women and girls who were systematically trafficked and abused by Jeffrey Epstein. Regarding Peter Mandelson’s relationship with Epstein, he called for all documents provided to the Intelligence and Security Committee on Mandelson’s appointments to high office to be unredacted, and for the Cabinet Secretary to review the appointment procedures to explain why they failed so catastrophically in this case. Image Jesse Norman calls out £8.2bn–£9bn cost of Government U-turns, and falling Police numbers 29 January 2026 Shadow Leader Jesse Norman exposes the high cost of Government U-turns since July 2024 which have created economic uncertainty

headings: h3: Invitations, Press Enquiries and other questions
pages.text_excerpt: Contact | Jesse Norman Skip to main content Main navigation Home About In Parliament News Articles Contact Search Cleaning Up the Wye NMITE User account menu Log in Toggle navigation Main navigation Home About In Parliament News Articles Contact Search Cleaning Up the Wye NMITE Contact Jesse For Constituency Casework Enter your Postcode below: If you need Jesse's assistance with any matter, please enter your postcode in the box above and select 'Jesse Norman' on the following page. Alternatively, you can call the office on 01432 276422 . If Jesse is not your MP, you will be able to contact your own MP from that page. Please ensure you include your full address and a contact telephone number , even if you have contacted Jesse before . We will not be able to offer you assistance until this has been provided. This is due to strict parliamentary protocol that states MPs may only act on behalf of their own constituents. You can also write to Jesse Norman MP, Suite 2A, Penn House, Broad Street, Hereford HR4 9AP . The constituency office is open Monday to Friday, 9am to 5pm . Protecting Your Data My office will not use your personal data in any way that goes beyond your reasonable expectations in contacting me. Please see my Privacy Notice . Invitations, Press Enquiries and other questions Please e-mail jesse.norman.mp@parliament.uk , call Jesse's Westminster office on 0207 219 7084 , or write to Jesse Norman MP, House of Commons, London SW1A 0AA . Jesse Norman MP Suite 2A, Penn House Broad Street Hereford HR4 9AP. Tel: 01432 276422 Contact Copyright © 2025. All rights reserved.

pages.title: Cleaning Up the Wye Campaign | Jesse Norman
pages.text_excerpt: Cleaning Up the Wye Campaign | Jesse Norman Skip to main content Main navigation Home About In Parliament News Articles Contact Search Cleaning Up the Wye NMITE User account menu Log in Toggle navigation Main navigation Home About In Parliament News Articles Contact Search Cleaning Up the Wye NMITE Cleaning Up the Wye Campaign Phosphate pollution in the River Wye is a very serious issue, destroying the fragile ecosystem and habitats along this gorgeous river, with huge implications for local businesses, tourism and public health. Building on the work of Councillor Elissa Swinglehurst and the Wye and Usk Foundation, I was the first to highlight this issue, in September 2020 , press the agencies for a collective response , and then call for a cross border, all-catchment collective long-term action plan . In June 2021, working with Bill Wiggin MP, Fay Jones MP, Mark Harper MP and David Davies MP, I called the national agencies and the local councils on both sides of the border to a special meeting devoted to this issue. In October 2021 I coordinated a letter from Wye catchment MPs to the Chief Secretary to the Treasury, calling for a specific spending package devoted to the Wye in the 2021 three year Spending Review. The result — finally, lockdowns and other delays notwithstanding — was the Shell Store meeting of July 2022 and the set-up of the Wye Phosphates Working Group, whose terms of reference have now been adopted — very much with my support — by the important Phosphates Commission , established by the four local authorities. Ministers are not able to speak for themselves in Parliament. But as a backbencher, I highlighted the plight of the River Wye in separate Parliamentary Questions to the Prime Minister , the Deputy Prime Minister and the Secretary of State for DEFRA . Among other things, I called for the proceeds of fines on water companies to be ring-fenced and devoted to a new National Rivers Recovery Fund . In November 2022 the Government accepted the ring-fencing , which is a major win. I have also asked the new Office of Environmental Protection to review the situation with the Wye, and expect to meet with them shortly. I finally, have written extensively on this issue, both in the local and national press , including via the links below. Solutions needed urgently to save “true national treasure” River Wye from pollution 24 February 2025 The River Wye is one of the most beautiful and ecologically significant rivers in the UK. Jesse Norman calls on the Government to back the River Wye Action Plan 8 October 2024 Jesse Norman takes the opportunity of an Opposition Day debate on farming to call on the Government to ensure that the £35 million allocated for the River Wye Action Plan is retained and Rive

pages.title: Jesse Norman urges Government action on Wiggin pension injustice to force trustees to pay top-ups from surpluses | Jesse Norman
headings: h3: Jesse Norman (Hereford and South Herefordshire) (Con)
h3: The Parliamentary Under-Secretary of State for Work and Pensions (Torsten Bell)
pages.text_excerpt: Jesse Norman urges Government action on Wiggin pension injustice to force trustees to pay top-ups from surpluses | Jesse Norman Skip to main content Main navigation Home About In Parliament News Articles Contact Search Cleaning Up the Wye NMITE User account menu Log in Toggle navigation Main navigation Home About In Parliament News Articles Contact Search Cleaning Up the Wye NMITE 19 March 2026 Jesse Norman urges Government action on Wiggin pension injustice to force trustees to pay top-ups from surpluses Jesse Norman urges the Government to intervene in the longstanding injustice perpetrated on pensioners of the Special Metals Wiggin pension fund, by requiring the trustees to use the scheme’s surpluses to provide top-up payments to those affected. Jesse Norman (Hereford and South Herefordshire) (Con) I congratulate the hon. Member for North Durham (Luke Akehurst) on his excellent speech and on securing this debate, and all those who have participated in it. I thank the Minister for allowing me a few words. On your behalf, Madam Deputy Speaker, I will mention your constituent Steve Mawby, because he has been a key campaigner in this important and very difficult area. I first got involved in it before I became a Member of Parliament in 2007, and I pay particular tribute to the late Roy Harries and Les Collard, because they brought to my attention the injustice that had been perpetrated on older pensioners by the Special Metals Wiggin pension fund. Hundreds of people were affected by that, and, alas, many of them are now dead. They were almost invariably widows, who were left receiving half of a much-diminished pension. These pensioners have not received a single penny in top-up payments since the legislation kicked in, because the company’s trustees have decided not to pay any discretionary bonuses at any point. The result is that those pensioners’ incomes are 55% lower than they would have been, and, for widows, half of that. It is an absolute outrage and a great historical injustice, and as the hon. Member says, the fact that an injustice occurred in the past does not mean that it is not important to address it now. Of course, there was a clear blunder in the legislation. I have raised this issue in the House over the years, and it is a matter of great embarrassment that previous Conservative Administrations and the coalition Government did not address it, but there is now the opportunity to do so. On the Special Metals Wiggin pension fund, I recognise that the trustees are often directly or indirectly controlled by the company, regardless of whether they are formally independent. The company has not paid any bonuses or top-ups, and it is now owned by a large American company, Precision Castparts Corp., which is itself owned by Warren Buffett and Berkshire Hathaway. I have written to Buffett, who has said that he is not willing to help either. It is a source of great sadness to me that even such a great philanthropist and entrepreneur such should have failed to do so. My question to the Minister is this: given that the Special Metals Wiggin pension fund has been in surplus for many years, could he, as well as considering the question of historical injustice, consider the question of whether the trustees should be required, out of the surpluses they have, to pay top-ups to the pensioners? I hope they will consider doing so retrospectively but, if not, they should do so in way that is generous, that recognises the terrible injustice that has been done, and that pays proper tribute to Les Collard, Roy Harries and all the great men and women of the Wiggin pensioners, who we justly celebrate and whose injustice we seek to fight. Hansard Interventions on the Minister Jesse Norman I will not detain the Minister long. If a group of trustees never pays a discretionary bonus, even though the scheme is in surplus, it starts to look like it is a policy of theirs to discriminate against a subset of their beneficiaries, and I think that is
